task2.sensitivity_note: Spec-mandated m=199, seed=2026 gives p=0.015 (all rows) and p=0.115 (excluding suspicious). A sensitivity rerun with m=999, seed=7 gives p=0.036 and p=0.122 respectively; the all-rows result is significant at 0.05 but is not robust below ~0.01, and the signal disappears once the 3 suspicious rows are removed.

methods_note: Independent blind review; no project docs, src, or prior results files were read, and results/blind/_key.json was never opened. TASK 1: For each series, values were tested against an exchangeable (i.i.d.-given-marginal) null via m=999 random permutations of the values (numpy default_rng, seed=12345 reset per series); p = (1+#{null>=obs})/(m+1). Four complementary statistics, all implemented from scratch: (1) Ljung-Box-style pooled autocorrelation, sum_{k=1..10} n*r_k^2 (one-sided large); (2) Wald-Wolfowitz runs test above/below the median, two-sided as |runs - null mean|; (3) Fisher's g spectral statistic, max periodogram ordinate / sum (one-sided large, detects periodicity); (4) absolute lag-1 Spearman rank autocorrelation (robust to nonlinearity/outliers). Verdict rule fixed before inspecting results: STRUCTURED if any raw p < 0.0125 (Bonferroni 0.05/4) or >=2 tests with raw p < 0.05; BORDERLINE if exactly one test with 0.0125 <= p < 0.05; otherwise NO DETECTED STRUCTURE. Verdicts: B, F, G, H STRUCTURED (all four tests p<=0.009; B/F/G show very strong serial dependence and dominant spectral peaks, H weaker but unambiguous); A, C, D, E, I NO DETECTED STRUCTURE (no test below 0.05). Task 1 verdicts were written down before any Task 2 work. TASK 2: From datasets/pcso-lotto/data_draws_1yr_audited.csv, Grand Lotto 6/55 rows (156) were date-sorted and split into halves; per-number deviation vector = counts of 1..55 minus n_draws*6/55; statistic = Pearson correlation between the two halves' deviation vectors. Monte-Carlo null: m=199 pairs of independent uniform 6-of-55 without-replacement simulated halves of matching sizes (numpy default_rng, seed=2026), p = (1+#{null>=obs})/(m+1). All rows: corr=0.251, p=0.015. Excluding Status=='suspicious_or_needs_review' (3 rows): corr=0.154, p=0.115. Reviewer interpretation: the half-vs-half frequency association in Grand Lotto 6/55 is borderline-significant at best and hinges on 3 rows already flagged as suspicious; after excluding them it is fully consistent with uniform i.i.d. draws. Analysis scripts: results/blind/_indep_task1.py and results/blind/_indep_task2.py; raw outputs in results/_indep_task1.json and results/_indep_task2.json.
